LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANT IRA SHARLIP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT IRA SHARLIP, M.D.'S DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS REGARDING RULING.) COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS THE COURT'S RULING VERBATIM AND SUBMIT IT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Virgil Pryor, Esq. of Ouim Reyen & Pryor, Tel:415.392.8300, for defendant and moving party;
Anthony Fruciano, Tel:415.533.3459, plaintiff and opposing party appearing on his own behalf.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT IRA SHARLIP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendant Ira Sharlip, M.D.'s demurrer to the cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend.

The court will hear this demurrer even though defendant failed to provide sufficient notice in accordance with Code of Civ. Proc., Sec. 1005. This demurer was originally filed in December 2018 and was fully briefed before the court took it off calendar in May 2019. Since all of the parties have already addressed the merits of the demurrer, there is no point in taking it off calendar again. (See Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697 ["It is well settled that the appearance of a party at the hearing of a motion and his or her opposition to the motion on its merits is a waiver of any defects or irregularities in the notice of motion. This rule applies even when no notice was given at all."].)

"Whether a defendant's conduct can reasonably be found to be outrageous is a question of law that must initially be determined by the court; if reasonable persons may differ, it is for the jury to determine whether the conduct was, in fact, outrageous. [citation] Ordinarily, a medical diagnosis and treatment advice will not be considered outrageous unless they are false and given in bad faith." (Berkley v. Dowds (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 518, 533-534 [affirming order sustaining demurrer to intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action where defendant physician urged plaintiff to terminate husband's life support to reduce costs].)

In this case, plaintiff fails to plead outrageous conduct as a matter of law. "Liability has been found only where the conduct has been so outrageous in character, and so extreme in degree, as to go beyond all possible bounds of decency, and to be regarded as atrocious, and utterly intolerable in a civilized community." (Cochran v. Cochran (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 488, 496.) Plaintiff's complaint does not allege such intolerable conduct and rude, callous or unprofessional conduct cannot support the claim.

[Defendant has 20 days to answer the remaing cause of action.]

Counsel for defendant shall prepare a proposed order which repeats the Court's ruling verbatim and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT IRA SHARLIP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant Ira Sharlip's demurrer is ordered OFF CALENDAR for failure to comply with CCP 430.41, which requires a "meet and confer in person or by telephone;" a writing and one voicemail message are not such a meet and confer. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately. Defendant Sharlip's response is due on June 14, 2019.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153 email: lnajera@sftc.org, Reported. (302/RBU)
